children.name: RegisterPatient
children.purpose: Register a new patient in the system
children.behavior: Extract patient details from input, validate uniqueness, store in patients data store, and return success or error
postconditions: patient is stored in patients data store

internal_leaf_accesses.reason: Check uniqueness and store new patient record

outputs.name: output
outputs.description: JSON with success status, patient ID, and message

global_vars.description: Check uniqueness and store new patient record

data_operations.description: Check uniqueness and store new patient record

acceptance_criteria.description: Patient is successfully registered and stored

children.name: BookAppointment
children.purpose: Book an appointment for a registered patient
children.behavior: Extract patient ID and appointment details, verify patient exists, check slot availability, store appointment, and return confirmation
postconditions: appointment is stored in appointments data store

call_inputs.name: input
call_inputs.description: JSON with patient ID and appointment details

internal_leaf_accesses.reason: Check slot availability and store new appointment

outputs.name: output
outputs.description: JSON with success status, appointment ID, and message

global_vars.description: Check slot availability and store new appointment

data_operations.description: Check slot availability and store new appointment

acceptance_criteria.description: Appointment is successfully booked and stored

children.name: GetRecords
children.purpose: Retrieve medical records for a patient
children.behavior: Extract patient ID, verify patient exists, fetch records from patients data store, and return records

internal_leaf_accesses.reason: Verify patient exists and fetch medical records

outputs.name: output
outputs.description: JSON with success status, records data, and message

global_vars.description: Verify patient exists and fetch medical records

data_operations.description: Verify patient exists and fetch medical records

acceptance_criteria.description: Records are successfully retrieved and returned

children.name: UpdateProfile
children.behavior: Extract patient ID and updated fields, verify patient exists, update profile in patients data store, and return confirmation
preconditions: input contains valid fields to update
postconditions: patient profile is updated in patients data store

call_inputs.name: input
call_inputs.description: JSON with patient ID and updated profile fields

internal_leaf_accesses.reason: Verify patient exists and update profile fields

outputs.name: output
outputs.description: JSON with success status, updated profile, and message

global_vars.description: Verify patient exists and update profile fields

data_operations.description: Verify patient exists and update profile fields

acceptance_criteria.description: Profile is successfully updated and stored

governance_notes: All children derive global_vars and data_operations directly from internal_leaf_accesses. Each resource/op pair in internal_leaf_accesses appears in both global_vars and data_operations. No exceptions needed. node_type is set to 'atomic_operation' for children that write to data stores, and 'pure_function' for GetRecords which only reads. Constraints and acceptance criteria are derived from preconditions and guarantees.

resource_audit.signature_preservation_detail: All signatures remain unchanged from Stage 2: RegisterPatient(input: dict) -> dict, BookAppointment(input: dict) -> dict, GetRecords(input: dict) -> dict, UpdateProfile(input: dict) -> dict. No modifications.
resource_audit.internal_leaf_mapping_detail: Each child's internal_leaf_accesses are mapped 1:1 to global_vars and data_operations. RegisterPatient: patients.read_write. BookAppointment: patients.read, appointments.read_write. GetRecords: patients.read. UpdateProfile: patients.read_write.
resource_audit.global_vars_data_operations_sync_detail: For each child, global_vars and data_operations contain identical entries. Each resource/op pair appears in both arrays with matching descriptions. No discrepancies.
resource_audit.coverage_detail: All resources referenced in internal_leaf_accesses (patients, appointments) are covered in global_vars and data_operations. No resource is missing. The 'appointments' resource only appears in BookAppointment, which is correct.
